profiles.name: Kentrosaurus
profiles.description: Forest dwelling, Kentrosaurus is rarely seen out in open areas unless it's to relax in the sun and rest with group members.  Male Kentrosaurus will sometimes put on displays to attract females or assert dominance in a group.  Due to a strong parental bond, young Kentrosaurus often stick with their f

scavenging: - Kentrosaurus does not tolerate scavengers; they will be vocally aggressive and use mock attacks to ward off scavengers.

Gondwa: Savannah, Lower Green Valley, Broken Tooth Canyon

profiles.name: Anodontosaurus
profiles.description: When settled down into a claimed territory with a mix-herd, Anodontosaurus will shed its colors to match the new environment, shedding to various greens of the forest or browns and grays to match canyons and deserts.  Mainly day time travelers, they will prefer to travel to new locations at dawn and
scavenging: - Anodontosaurus do not perceive scavengers as a potential threat.
nesting: Season: Dry
Gondwa: Burned Forest, Hunters Thicket, Titans Pass, Young Grove
Panjura: Clubtail Canyon, Tyrant's Gorge, The Highlands, Two Brother's Hollow, Cliffside Retreat
specialRules: Aquaphobic: Anodontosaurus **must not** enter water after combat has started.

Claustrophobic: Anodontosaurus *may* be **combat aggressive** to **adult low-tier**, and **up to adult apex** herbivores not in their group within **tail swing** range until the herbivores flee. Anodontosaurus **must not** pursue fleeing herbivores.
Survivor: **Adolescent and below** Anodontosaurus **must flee** from adult mid tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted. -
rpNotes: Leadership: Independent - Will think of their own personal benefit before others.

Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in an Anodontosaurus only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

profiles.name: Peloroplites
profiles.description: Peloroplites, despite their size and fearsome spikes, are a reclusive species, rarely venturing from the forests they call home. Their tough beaks allow them to forage on tough vegetation and roots that other species tend to avoid, which lets them coexist peacefully with most herbivores. Peloroplite

scavenging: - Peloroplites do not perceive scavengers as a potential threat.

Panjura: Clubtail Canyon, The Highlands, Two Brother's Hollow
specialRules: Aquaphobic: Peloroplites **must not** enter water after combat has started.
Survivor: **Adolescent and below** Peloroplites **must flee** from adult mid tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

profiles.description: Parasaurolophus, despite their quite timid and careful character, form big and, definitely dangerous, herds that consist of numerous individuals of both genders. profiles.name: Edmontosaurus Annectens
profiles.description: Male Edmontosaurus tend to stay at the rear of the group while traveling, protecting the rear of the herd while the female Edmontosaurus leads. profiles.description: They are not afraid to cross rivers or other bodies of water if it means herd safety and food. profiles.name: Garrigatitan
profiles.description: Garrigatitan often preferred a solitary lifestyle. They were known to become aggressive when sharing food with other herbivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee away from the Garrigatitan or its herd, Garrigatitan *may* become combat aggressive until the scavengers flee from Garrigatitan and its herd.
nesting: Season: Dry
Gondwa: Burned Forest, Hoodoo Expanse, Hotsprings
Panjura: Aperture Pass, Grassland Lake, Sleeping Giant

Survivor: **Adolescent and below** Garrigatitan **must flee** from adult sub-apex apex and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted. -
